commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Manual review is appropriate because the remaining blocker is maintainer acceptance of retained npm generation lifetime, not a narrow mechanical repair.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff changes managed npm layout and marker handling without adding dependencies, workflows, action refs, lockfiles, or lifecycle scripts. (sha=6f239c3569dd40821c7016dfb00875d75911a712)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes managed npm plugin updates to install into generation-specific project roots, retain replaced npm generations for live imports, and skip retained generations during registry recovery and doctor cleanup.

PR surface: Source +495, Tests +956. Total +1451 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level review: current main can remove the previous managed npm install directory after recording a new install, and the PR body links real Windows stale-import logs. I did not run a fresh live reproduction in this read-only pass.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is appropriate because the remaining blocker is maintainer acceptance of retained npm generation lifetime, not a narrow mechanical repair.

target.title: fix: npm plugin updates break running gateway imports
target.body:
﻿Related: #85844

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users updating managed npm/ClawHub plugins could keep a running gateway pointed at old hashed `dist` chunks while the updater removed that old package tree. In the observed Windows 2026.6.10-beta.1 failure, the gateway later failed to talk to the user because Node could not import a stale `run-attempt-*.js` file from the previous `@openclaw/codex` install.

This PR addresses the managed npm plugin replacement slice of #85844. It does not try to solve every stale core-dist or process-restart lifecycle case.

## Why This Change Was Made

Managed npm updates now install into artifact-generation project roots instead of replacing the previous package directory in place. Replaced npm generations stay on disk for live imports, but OpenClaw records an OpenClaw-owned retained marker during the install-record commit so registry recovery and doctor repair do not treat them as active installs.

The marker state is kept in the same commit/rollback path as install records: old generations are marked only after the next records are written, active retained generations are unmarked when committed, and marker changes are restored if the config commit rolls back. Install/update policy mode remains tied to the user-visible operation, so retained-only generations do not turn fresh installs into policy updates.

Retention policy in this PR is compatibility-first: replaced managed npm generations are intentionally kept until an explicit operator/future cleanup policy removes them, because a running gateway can still hold old module URLs. The retained marker is the boundary that keeps those compatibility generations out of active registry recovery and destructive doctor cleanup.

## User Impact

Users can update an npm-backed plugin without breaking already-running code that still imports chunks from the previous plugin generation. After restart or recovery, OpenClaw sees only the committed active generation; retained old generations are ignored by registry recovery and protected from destructive doctor cleanup.

Operators still get install-policy semantics: retained-only package trees do not make a fresh `install` or forced update look like a real update.

## Evidence

Real before evidence: #85844 includes a Windows 2026.6.10-beta.1 gateway failure where `@openclaw/codex` tried to import a missing stale hashed dist chunk after update: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/85844#issuecomment-4761855575

Live managed-npm update proof on Windows source checkout, isolated state, 2026-06-21 UTC:

- Created a local fixture npm registry with `@openclaw/proof-plugin@1.0.0` and `@openclaw/proof-plugin@1.0.1`.
- Created isolated `HOME`, `USERPROFILE`, `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, `NPM_CONFIG_CACHE`, and `NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG` under temp directories.
- Installed v1, verified runtime import and `proof.ping` gateway method registration, started a real loopback gateway, updated to v2 while that gateway was running, then verified both the old retained generation and the new active generation remain importable.

Key redacted terminal excerpt:

```text
COMMAND: install npm proof plugin v1
Installing @openclaw/proof-plugin@1.0.0 into <temp-state>\.openclaw\npm\projects\openclaw-proof-plugin-e727ac5dd4...
Pinned npm install record to @openclaw/proof-plugin@1.0.0.
Installed plugin: proof-plugin
EXIT(install npm proof plugin v1): 0

COMMAND: runtime inspect proof plugin before update
EXIT(runtime inspect proof plugin before update): 0
runtimeImportedBefore=True
runtimeGatewayMethodsBefore=proof.ping
oldRecordedVersion=1.0.0
oldRecordedSpec=@openclaw/proof-plugin@1.0.0

COMMAND: import old generation before update
{"id":"proof-plugin","name":"Proof Plugin","path":"<old-generation>\\index.js"}
EXIT(import old generation before update): 0

gatewayPid=146860
gatewayReadyBeforeUpdate=HTTP 200: {"ready":true,"failing":[]...}

COMMAND: update proof plugin to v2 while gateway is running
Installing @openclaw/proof-plugin@1.0.1 into <temp-state>\.openclaw\npm\projects\openclaw-proof-plugin-e727ac5dd4__openclaw-generation__openclaw-proof-plugin-1.0.1-openclaw-proof-plugin--594379edef...
Updated proof-plugin: 1.0.0 -> 1.0.1.
Restart the gateway to load plugins and hooks.
EXIT(update proof plugin to v2 while gateway is running): 0

gatewayReadyAfterUpdate=HTTP 200: {"ready":true,"failing":[]...}

COMMAND: runtime inspect proof plugin after update
EXIT(runtime inspect proof plugin after update): 0
runtimeImportedAfter=True
runtimeGatewayMethodsAfter=proof.ping
newRecordedVersion=1.0.1
newRecordedSpec=@openclaw/proof-plugin@1.0.1

COMMAND: import old generation after update
{"exists":true,"id":"proof-plugin","name":"Proof Plugin","path":"<old-generation>\\index.js"}
EXIT(import old generation after update): 0

COMMAND: import new active generation after update
{"exists":true,"id":"proof-plugin","name":"Proof Plugin","path":"<new-generation>\\index.js"}
EXIT(import new active generation after update): 0

managedProjectDirs=2
managedProjectDir=<temp-state>\.openclaw\npm\projects\openclaw-proof-plugin-e727ac5dd4
managedProjectDir=<temp-state>\.openclaw\npm\projects\openclaw-proof-plugin-e727ac5dd4__openclaw-generation__openclaw-proof-plugin-1.0.1-openclaw-proof-plugin--594379edef
RESULT: PASS - running gateway survived npm plugin update, active record rotated to v2, old managed generation remained importable
```

Focused after-patch validation:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts` (52 passed, 3 skipped)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/install.test.ts` (106 passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/plugins-install-record-commit.test.ts` (13 passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/plugins-install-persist.test.ts` (15 passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.test.ts` (21 passed, 1 skipped)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.test.ts` (11 passed)

Static validation:

- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.ts src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.test.ts src/cli/plugins-install-record-commit.ts src/cli/plugins-install-record-commit.test.ts src/cli/plugins-install-persist.ts src/cli/plugins-install-persist.test.ts src/plugins/install-paths.ts src/plugins/install.ts src/plugins/install.test.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/plugins/installed-plugin-index-record-reader.ts src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.test.ts src/plugins/managed-npm-retention.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.ts src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.test.ts src/cli/plugins-install-record-commit.ts src/cli/plugins-install-record-commit.test.ts src/cli/plugins-install-persist.ts src/cli/plugins-install-persist.test.ts src/plugins/install-paths.ts src/plugins/install.ts src/plugins/install.test.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/plugins/installed-plugin-index-record-reader.ts src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.test.ts src/plugins/managed-npm-retention.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo`

Review closeout:

- `$env:PYTHONUTF8='1'; python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local`
- Result: `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`

Not rerun in this branch: a full published-package upgrade from npm. The live proof above uses the source checkout plus a local fixture registry to exercise the same managed npm install-record, generation-retention, runtime import, and running-gateway update path without touching the user's real OpenClaw state.
target.author: ooiuuii
